question: Does the generated image depict a snowboarder wearing an orange shell jacket, black pants, and a helmet, matching the visual characteristics of the reference image?
Does the generated image include a Husky dog with gray, white, and black fur markings that matches the appearance of the reference animal?
Is the stainless steel insulated tumbler with a black cap clearly recognizable and matching the reference object?
Is the background recognizable as the wide-angle ski resort scene from the reference image?
Is the snowboarder positioned in the left-front area of the frame as specified in the sketch?
Is the Husky positioned on the right side of the frame, appearing to chase the snowboarder?
Is the tumbler placed in the lower-right foreground of the composition?
Does the snowboarder retain realistic snow spray indicating a dynamic turn?
Are there visible snow powder splashes and continuous footprints at the Husky's feet?
Does the tumbler appear half-buried in the snow with visible compression marks at the edges?
Are there visible snowboard tracks on the snow surface connecting plausibly to the snowboarder's path?
Do the metallic reflections on the tumbler reflect the snowy ski resort environment rather than the dark studio background from the reference?
Do the cast shadows of the snowboarder, dog, and tumbler follow a single consistent light source direction?
Do the shadows cast on the snow surface have a realistic light blue tint?
Is the depth of field unified such that the subjects are sharp, the foreground tumbler is slightly sharp, and the background is blurred?
Are the subjects (snowboarder and dog) free from anatomical distortions such as extra limbs or twisted joints?
Is the tumbler free from geometric distortions or warping?
Are there any obvious visual artifacts or unnatural blending edges around the subjects?
Does the overall image exhibit advertisement-quality realism with appropriate noise and contrast?
